title: bartlettina calderonii
intro: Bartlettina calderonii, also known as Mexican mint or yerba de la luna, is a plant species in the sunflower family that is native to Mexico and Central America. Traditionally, Bartlettina calderonii has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, making it effective in treating wounds, fever, and skin conditions. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing stress, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Bartlettina calderonii often involve infusing the leaves in hot water to make a tea, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
